business.headline: Gita Gopinath flags inflation, more fuel hikes likely; says rupee at 100 no worryAs the West Asia conflict drags on, Gita Gopinath warns India may face sharper fuel hikes, rising inflation and a weaker rupee, while arguing the country still has enough economic strength to avoid panic.

Gita Gopinath discusses impact of West Asia conflict, rising oil prices and moreIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Gita Gopinath, Professor of Economics at Harvard University, discusses the impact of the West Asia conflict and rising oil prices on the Indian economy.

Sebi plans PF, NPS-like payroll-linked MF SIPs. Details hereInvesting in mutual funds through salary deductions, much like PF and NPS, may soon become possible. SEBI has proposed a new framework that could also allow donations through mutual fund units.

Pleasantly surprised: Parle VP reacts after PM Modi gifts Melody to MeloniPrime Minister Narendra Modi's gift of Melody toffee to Giorgia Meloni during his Italy trip gave fresh attention to the 'Melodi' buzz, leaving Parle Products pleasantly surprised by the global attention.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite rupee hitting fresh record lowThe BSE Sensex rose 117.54 points, or 0.16%, to close at 75,318.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 41 points, or 0.17%, to settle at 23,659.

Gold loans surge 50% in FY26: Why more Indians are borrowing against goldNeed money urgently but do not want the hassle of lengthy paperwork? Many Indians are increasingly turning to gold loans as a quick and easier way to raise funds, especially at a time when gold prices remain strong.

Parle Industries jumps 5% after Modi-Meloni Melody buzz, but there's a catchSocial media buzz around PM Modi gifting Melody to Italy PM Giorgia Meloni appears to have sparked confusion among retail investors, sending Parle Industries shares sharply higher despite no business connection to the toffee brand. Parle Products is the firm producing the Melody toffee.

Banks may soon have to share more details on risks and financial health, says RBIOn Tuesday, the RBI released a draft circular, wherein banks will be required to publish more detailed information on important financial measures such as capital adequacy, leverage, liquidity and risks.

Need PF money fast? EPFO may soon let you withdraw funds through UPILabour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya said on Tuesday that the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has completed testing for a facility that would allow members to withdraw their Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) using the UPI payment gateway.

A falling rupee is revealing a bigger crisis of fleeing moneyHandcrafted ByOur Editors The falling rupee needs no support at the moment, and foreign exchange reserves shouldn't be burnt. However, the falling rupee might be pointing to a deeper investment problem. Everything explained here in layman's terms.

Rupee hits record low, inches closer to 97 against US dollarThe rupee fell to a record 96.88 against the US dollar as higher Treasury yields, firm crude and a stronger dollar hit sentiment. The move sharpened concerns around imported inflation, market volatility and pressure on India's import bill.

Sensex, Nifty down: Why is stock market falling today?The BSE Sensex fell 389.19 points, or 0.52%, to 74,811.66 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 107.90 points, or 0.46%, to 23,510.10 as of 9:39 am.

Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty below 23,500; Tata Steel falls 2%The BSE Sensex fell 389.19 points, or 0.52%, to 74,811.66 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 107.90 points, or 0.46%, to 23,510.10 as of 9:39 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Stock markets are set for a weaker open as rising crude, higher US yields and Iran tensions weigh on sentiment. Persistent foreign selling, elevated volatility and key technical levels are keeping traders cautious.

Wait 5 years for gratuity or take the pay hike? Here's what Indian workers saidYou are just months away from gratuity when a recruiter calls with a tempting pay hike. Do you wait for guaranteed money, or take the career leap? To understand what people really choose, I spoke to Indian professionals across sectors, and the answers were surprisingly divided.

ICICI Bank hikes debit card DCC fee from 1% to 3.5%: What it meansDynamic Currency Conversion, commonly called DCC, allows customers to pay in their home currency instead of the local currency while making international transactions.

3 in 10 loan applications show ITR-bank mismatch: Can you still get a personal loan?Your ITR says one thing, but your bank statement tells a slightly different story — should you worry? As lenders tighten checks and nearly 3 in 10 loan applications show income mismatches, can you still get a personal loan without trouble? Let's find out here.

Why foreign investors are shunning India: Surjit Bhalla points out root causeEven as India's economy faces mounting pressure from rising crude oil prices and disruptions in fertiliser and energy supplies, economist and author Surjit Bhalla has identified a worsening investment climate as the country's biggest challenge.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as banking drag offsets IT rally; rupee hits record lowThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 114.19 points, or 0.15%, to close at 75,200.85, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 31.95 points, or 0.14%, to settle at 23,618.

Govt likely to receive a big dividend from RBI in FY 27The Reserve Bank of India is expected to approve a record surplus transfer to the government this week. The payout could give the exchequer extra room as higher energy prices add fresh uncertainty.

entertainment.headline: Zaheer Iqbal's 'Melody' joke with Sonakshi Sinha wins the internet Sonakshi Sinha and Zaheer Iqbal recreated the viral Melody meme in a playful Instagram video. The post added to the online frenzy around the toffee trend, courtesy PM Narendra Modi.

Did Karan Kundrra's ex Anusha Dandekar react to his proposal? Internet thinks so Reddit users were convinced that Anusha Dandekar reacted to ex-boyfriend Karan Kundrra's proposal to Tejasswi Prakash. Soon after the proposal aired, she shared a cryptic Instagram post about "thanking god".

Avengers: Doomsday may be Marvel's biggest shake-up yet: Russo Brothers The Russo Brothers say Avengers: Doomsday will be a complete reinvention with unexpected twists and tone. They say the film will carry forward the emotional conflict and complexity that powered Captain America: Civil War.

Jeethu Joseph reveals what he got wrong while writing Drishyam Ahead of Drishyam 3's release, director Jeethu Joseph reflected on the real-life incident that inspired the franchise and how he believes audiences sided too strongly with Mohanlal's Georgekutty.

It's now and forever: Karan Kundrra proposes to Tejasswi Prakash on Desi Bling Karan Kundrra proposed to Tejasswi Prakash on Netflix's Desi Bling, with the cast helping stage the surprise. The moment left her in tears and sent TejRan fans into celebration online.

Anurag Dobhal recalls 'humiliating' hospital stay, alleges new complaint by family YouTuber Anurag Dobhal has alleged that his family is "conspiring" against him again months after his car crash and interfaith marriage controversy. The influencer also opened up about a difficult and deeply personal phase during his recovery.

Saif honours father Tiger Pataudi with cricketer Ian Botham: He was my hero Actor Saif Ali Khan recalled growing up around cricket and learning life lessons from his father, cricketer Tiger Pataudi, at the memorial lecture in Kolkata. He was joined by cricketer Ian Botham at the event.

Jr NTR introduces pet macaws Asahi and Yuuhi on his birthday Jr NTR marked his 43rd birthday with a special Instagram post introducing his pet macaws, Asahi and Yuuhi. The meaningful names and his connection with Japan quickly caught fans' attention online.

CM Vijay meets Tamil directors' union, KS Ravikumar and Venkat Prabhu share pics Several members of the Tamil film directors' union met Chief Minister Vijay on 19 May and conveyed their wishes. The interaction followed fresh industry outreach, including Kamal Haasan's demands and Rajmohan's new portfolios.

Jr NTR calls fans his biggest blessing in thank-you note after Dragon glimpse Actor Jr NTR thanked fans for the strong response to the Dragon glimpse and credited director Prashanth Neel for envisioning the film. The note came as the teaser gained momentum online ahead of his birthday and sharpened interest in the project.

Disha Patani's sister Khushboo slams dowry abuse after Noida, Bhopal deaths Khushboo Patani, actor Disha Patani's sister's hard-hitting posts on alleged dowry deaths, marriage pressure and women being told to "adjust" in abusive situations has gone viral. Here's what she said and why it's grabbing attention.

Amaal Mallik says 'big boys' got him out of 60 projects, apologises to fans Singer-composer Amaal Mallik claimed powerful people in the film and music industry pushed him out of more than 60 projects over the last eight years. In a long note on X, he revealed why audiences hear less of his music in films now.

Jack Ryan Ghost War review: A spy thriller for the age of silent warsRelease Date:Wed May 20, 2026 Tom Clancy's Jack Ryan: Ghost War brings John Krasinski back into a covert mission shaped by rogue operatives and hidden enemies. The film feels timely because modern conflict now unfolds through data, surveillance and invisible pressure points.

Samay Raina was super nervous during Latent 2 shoot with Alia Bhatt, Sharvari A viral social media post has sparked buzz around India's Got Latent Season 2, with Alia Bhatt and Sharvari appearing in the first episode. Now, an insider also claimed Samay Raina seemed nervous at first before settling into his usual rhythm.

Off Campus vs The Summer I Turned Pretty: Okay fans, gather From fake dating and hockey drama to slow-burn heartbreak at Cousins Beach, here's why Off Campus and The Summer I Turned Pretty are battling for the young adult romance crown.

Why RJ Balaji insisted on a May release for Suriya's Karuppu As Karuppu races towards Rs 200 crore, RJ Balaji revealed why he rejected multiple release dates and waited months to bring Suriya's film to theatres in May.

Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed review: Bad decisions never looked this bingeableRelease Date:Wed May 20, 2026 Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed review: Tatiana Maslany stars as Paula, a divorced mother drawn into an online attack, blackmail and murder. The Apple TV series uses that spiral to explore loneliness, desperation and digital-age distrust.

Janhvi Kapoor plays cricket with Pat Cummins, knocks out bystander's phone at event Australian cricketer Pat Cummins bowled to Janhvi Kapoor during a playful cricket exchange at a Mumbai event. The clip caught attention online after her shot nearly hit a bystander's phone.

Gullak 5 trailer: Mishra family returns with new challenges and old warmth The trailer for Gullak Season 5 brings the Mishra family back ahead of its June 5 release on Sony LIV. It points to a season shaped by change at home, growing responsibilities and the pull of familiar roots.

general news.headline: PM calls crucial council meeting today, all ministers asked to stay in DelhiAll Union ministers have been asked to remain in Delhi ahead of a crucial meeting chaired by PM Modi, where governance, the West Asia crisis and possible political changes are expected to dominate discussions.

US can wait 'a few days' for 'right answers' from Iran, says TrumpTrump said the United States would wait a few days for Iran's response as talks entered what he called the final stage. Iran kept diplomatic channels open but warned of wider conflict if Washington launched fresh military action.

Meet the Cockroach who's launched India's newest political partyAbhijeet Dipke said the Cockroach Janta Party emerged after the Chief Justice's remarks angered young Indians online. He said the response reflected wider frustration among Gen Z over representation, jobs and political discourse.

Mohammad Amir gets British citizenship. Is ex-Pakistan pacer eligible for IPL?Former Pakistan fast bowler Mohammad Amir is now eligible to play in the IPL after acquiring British citizenship, potentially allowing him to enter the league as an overseas player from 2027.
India, Italy and the Melodi hai chocolateyBeyond the bilateral agreements, Modi and Meloni are in on the joke together. Melodi is adding grist to the meme mill.

India on weather edge: Scorching heat to continue on ThursdayAs North India bakes in one of the worst heat spells of the season, the South braces for more storms. India's weather department has warned the country of a day of weather extremes, with multiple alerts issued.

TVK scraps Rs 16.8 lakh water tank tender after DMK flags 'planned' 6-hour processA six-hour government tender for a water tank project in Tamil Nadu has snowballed into a political flashpoint, with the opposition alleging favouritism, rushed approvals and possible violations of tender transparency rules.
Reward on absconding husband raised in Twisha Sharma death caseBhopal Police have raised the reward on absconding accused Samarth Singh as raids continue in the Twisha Sharma death case.

No FIFA World Cup 2026 for Indian fans? Doordarshan says no to broadcast battlePrasar Bharati told the Delhi High Court it is not responsible for acquiring FIFA World Cup rights in India. The stand has deepened uncertainty over how Indian fans will watch the tournament before the June 12 start.

Delhi reports five murders in 24 hours, minors among victimsFive people were killed in three separate incidents across Delhi within 24 hours, including a shooting, a double stabbing and a fatal family clash.

US set up a chessboard with Munir as a pawn years ago. It's playing out nowA chessboard had been laid much earlier. A game had begun even before former Pakistan PM Imran Khan realised it, and Asim Munir took over as the army chief. The US moved the pieces for a regime change in Pakistan. It now has a strategically placed player as the Middle East burns.

Congress's 59-year exile in Tamil Nadu ends, two MLAs to join Vijay CabinetCongress MLAs Rajesh Kumar and P Vishwanathan will join Chief Minister Vijay's cabinet on Thursday. Their induction brings the party into a Tamil Nadu government for the first time in 59 years.

Pleasantly surprised: Parle VP reacts after PM Modi gifts Melody to MeloniPrime Minister Narendra Modi's gift of Melody toffee to Giorgia Meloni during his Italy trip gave fresh attention to the 'Melodi' buzz, leaving Parle Products pleasantly surprised by the global attention.

C++ creator says AI writes rubbish code, coders retiring to avoid dealing with itAI coding tools like Claude Code and Codex have changed the way coders work, with many companies pushing for more AI written code. However, according to the creator of C++, Bjarne Stroustrup, the AI written code is so bad that senior developers are retiring instead of dealing with it.

NEET paper leak: Bulldozer rolls at Pune coaching centre over Rs 3.2 crore tax duesThe Pune Municipal Corporation sealed RCC Classes premises linked to Shivraj Motegaonkar and began demolition action. The move followed claims of unpaid property tax, unauthorised use and a wider inquiry into linked properties.

Live: Iran signals openness to diplomacy while rejecting pressure tactics from TrumpUS-Israel-Iran War Updates: Tensions in the US-Iran standoff remained high as Tehran signalled openness to diplomacy while simultaneously warning that its forces were fully prepared for any future confrontation. Iranian officials said all maritime operations remained under the control of the IRGC navy and stressed that no military vessels would be allowed to transit through its waters. Pakistan continued its mediation efforts, with Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi travelling to Tehran for the second time in a week to hold talks with Iranian officials. Meanwhile, internet watchdog NetBlocks said Iran had entered its 82nd straight day of near-total internet blackout, with much of the country still cut off from the global web. Regional tensions also widened after the UAE demanded that Iraq immediately stop attacks allegedly launched from Iraqi territory following accusations that a drone targeting a UAE nuclear facility had originated there. On the ground, Israeli raids continued across the occupied West Bank, where 20 people were arrested near Jerusalem, while Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon killed at least 19 people and wounded 17 others. Oil prices, however, fell after US President Donald Trump again claimed the Iran war would end quickly. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Melody to Melodi: How a 1983 Indian candy turned into a diplomatic symbolLaunched in 1983 as an affordable caramel-chocolate toffee, Parle's Melody has survived changing snack trends for decades. Now, after PM Narendra Modi gifted it to Italy PM Giorgia Meloni in Rome, the Re 1 candy has transformed into a viral diplomatic symbol tied to the internet-famous "Melodi" meme.

60 days or leave: Indians with H-1B visa struggling to survive after layoffsThousands of Indians on H-1B visas are facing uncertainty after fresh layoffs at major tech firms like Meta and Amazon. With only 60 days to find a new job, many are now struggling to stay in the US amid tighter immigration scrutiny.

Twisha's body kept in -4°C storage for 7 days. The ideal temperature is way lowerEight days after her death, Twisha Sharma's body remains at the AIIMS Bhopal mortuary under less-than-ideal preservation conditions as no ultra-low temperature facility was available in the city. Bhopal court has directed police to identify and submit a report on such facilities in the state without delay.

Court rejects 2nd autopsy plea, seeks report on Twisha's body preservationA court has rejected a plea for a second postmortem in the Twisha Sharma death case, but concerns over preserving the body have triggered urgent directions to police and medical authorities.

Why Nepal is objecting to Kailash Mansarovar Yatra via Lipulekh PassNepal has objected to the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra via Lipulekh Pass, saying the route crosses territory it claims. The dispute traces back to the 1816 Treaty of Sugauli and competing claims over the Kali river's origin, making a religious journey part of a larger geopolitical contest.

Parle Industries jumps 5% after Modi-Meloni Melody buzz, but there's a catchSocial media buzz around PM Modi gifting Melody to Italy PM Giorgia Meloni appears to have sparked confusion among retail investors, sending Parle Industries shares sharply higher despite no business connection to the toffee brand. Parle Products is the firm producing the Melody toffee.

Iran builds new system of control in Hormuz with island checkpoints, fees: ReportIran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz, the conduit for about a fifth of the global oil supply, has thrown the world economy into turmoil.

Israeli police make Gaza flotilla activists kneel with hands tied, sparks outcryThe activists had been aboard a flotilla carrying humanitarian aid toward Gaza, where aid groups continue to warn of deep shortages despite a ceasefire agreement brokered by the United States in October 2025.

sports.headline: Hardik Pandya rues fielding collapse behind MI's disastrous IPL 2026 season IPL 2026: Hardik Pandya admitted Mumbai Indians' poor fielding standards became one of the biggest reasons behind their disastrous IPL 2026 campaign. After another sloppy performance against KKR, MI's season now heads towards an underwhelming finish with serious questions looming over the franchise.

Manish Pandey repays KKR faith with match-winning MI knock: That's why I'm here IPL 2026: Manish Pandey finally got his first real batting opportunity of IPL 2026 and instantly turned back the clock for KKR. On a tense Eden Gardens night, the veteran repaid Kolkata's faith with a vintage chase-masterclass that kept their playoff hopes alive.

IPL playoffs scenario: How Kolkata Knight Riders can still seal 4th spot IPL 2026, KKR vs MI: Kolkata Knight Riders have given themselves a great chance of qualifying for the playoffs after beating the Mumbai Indians by four wickets on Wednesday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Who came in for Angkrish Raghuvanshi vs MI? KKR's concussion sub sparks debate IPL 2026: KKR's handling of Angkrish Raghuvanshi's substitution during the win over Mumbai Indians triggered confusion. The lack of clarity over a reported concussion replacement turned the match into a rules debate.

KKR vs MI: A Manish Pandey classic at the Eden keeps Kolkata in playoff race Manish Pandey steadied Kolkata Knight Riders after an early wobble and carried the chase against Mumbai Indians to help them remain in the hunt for the IPL 2026 playoffs. MI's batting woes continued with the bat, and they struggled with their catching as Mumbai lost their ninth match of the season.

Couple goals: Gael Monfils, Elina Svitolina train together at French Open Tennis power couple Gael Monfils and Elina Svitolina thrilled fans after training together at Roland Garros, with their practice session quickly going viral on social media. Fresh off her Italian Open triumph, Svitolina heads into the French Open in top form, while Monfils is set to enjoy massive home support in Paris.

MS Dhoni taught me confidence without overconfidence: CSK star Dewald Brevis IPL 2026: Dewald Brevis said MS Dhoni's advice at CSK helped him understand the line between confidence and overconfidence. The South African batter said Dhoni's humility and presence remained influential despite his injury-hit season.
Chahar recreates note celebration after dismissing Finn Allen, but with a twist Deepak Chahar dismissed Finn Allen and then mimed his own version of the note celebration at Eden Gardens.

Rishabh Pant did nothing wrong: Kaif on wicketkeeper losing Test vice-captaincy Mohammed Kaif has criticised the selectors for removing Rishabh Pant as India's Test vice-captain before the Afghanistan series. He said using IPL form to judge a proven red-ball match-winner exposed inconsistency in selection thinking.

French Open tensions rise as players plan media protest over prize money structure Top tennis stars are planning a media protest at the French Open over prize-money distribution, with players reportedly limiting press interactions during Roland Garros. The protest comes as players push for a bigger share of Grand Slam revenues.

Mohun Bagan among seven clubs denied AIFF license: What happens now? AIFF's club licensing body rejected the Premier 1 applications of seven clubs, including Mohun Bagan Super Giant, for 2026-27. The decision leaves them temporarily ineligible for national and AFC competitions unless they secure clearance in time.

No FIFA World Cup 2026 for Indian fans? Doordarshan says no to broadcast battlePrasar Bharati told the Delhi High Court it is not responsible for acquiring FIFA World Cup rights in India. The stand has deepened uncertainty over how Indian fans will watch the tournament before the June 12 start.

Mohammad Amir gets British citizenship. Is ex-Pakistan pacer eligible for IPL?Former Pakistan fast bowler Mohammad Amir is now eligible to play in the IPL after acquiring British citizenship, potentially allowing him to enter the league as an overseas player from 2027.

We are not done: Pat Cummins makes bold claim on Australia pace trio's futureSpeaking to India Today, Pat Cummins said the legendary trio of himself, Mitchell Starc, and Josh Hazlewood have no plans to retire anytime soon, targeting a monumental upcoming calendar that includes the ODI World Cup, the Ashes in England and a Test series in India.

RCB should never take Virat Kohli for granted: Mo Bobat on star's impactVirat Kohli's runs continue to headline RCB's IPL 2026 campaign, but the franchise believes his biggest contribution still goes far beyond statistics. RCB Director of Cricket Mo Bobat called Kohli the emotional and cultural engine driving the defending champions forward.

Ranji Trophy to begin on October 11 as BCCI announces 1,788-match domestic scheduleThe BCCI has announced a 1,788-match domestic calendar for 2026-27 across senior and junior cricket. It restores key fixtures, turns select U-23 events into T20 format.

Would you treat Bumrah the same way as Shami? Ex-India batter slams Ajit AgarkarWasim Jaffer has criticised Ajit Agarkar and the BCCI after Mohammed Shami was left out again for the upcoming series against Afghanistan. He called the explanation disrespectful and questioned whether Jasprit Bumrah would be treated the same way.

Ex-US Open champion wins hearts with touching gesture for heartbroken rivalFormer US Open champion Bianca Andreescu earned widespread praise after consoling tearful French teenager Daphnee Mpetshi Perricard following their French Open qualifying clash in Paris.

Mohammed Shami acquitted in cheque bounce case filed by wife Hasin JahanAn Alipore court acquitted Mohammed Shami in the Rs 1 lakh cheque bounce case filed by wife Hasin Jahan. The ruling brings legal relief, even as their maintenance and alimony dispute continues in higher courts.

India slip further in WTC after Bangladesh's historic Test series win vs PakistanBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Bangladesh's historic 2-0 Test series sweep over Pakistan has shaken up the World Test Championship standings, with India slipping to sixth place amid an inconsistent campaign.

Your last Test as captain? Shan Masood irked by reporter's question in BangladeshBangladesh vs Pakistan, 2nd Test: Asked if this was his last Test as captain, Shan Masood stayed measured after Pakistan's humbling loss to Bangladesh in Sylhet on Wednesday. He urged focus on improving Test cricket, stressing the need not to enforce wholesale changes.

Phil Salt set to return as RCB get major boost before IPL 2026 playoffsPhil Salt is set to rejoin Royal Challengers Bengaluru this week after a month out with a finger injury. Salt has been out of action for almost a month after suffering an injury during a league game.

Sanju Samson quashes beef with Heinrich Klaasen after bust-up in CSK vs SRHSanju Samson and Heinrich Klaasen cooled tempers after their Chepauk exchange over a stumping dismissal. Samson and Klaasen expressed their admiration for each other with their posts on Instagram.

When and where to buy IPL 2026 playoffs, final tickets: Complete guideIrfan Pathan said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is beginning to create Chris Gayle-like fear after his 38-ball 93 against Lucknow Super Giants. The knock took the teenager to 53 sixes this season, putting Gayle's IPL record firmly under threat.

Like Gayle, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is going to be feared by bowlers: Irfan PathanFollowing an explosive 38-ball 93 against LSG, Rajasthan Royals' teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is drawing massive praise from former Indian stars Irfan Pathan and Sanjay Bangar, who see shades of a prime Chris Gayle in the 15-year-old.

Shreyas Iyer main contender for India T20I captaincy, says Ricky PontingIPL 2026: Ricky Ponting has positioned Shreyas Iyer as a leading contender for India's T20I captaincy despite Punjab Kings' slump. The defending world champions will tour Ireland and England for T20I series after the IPL.

Riyan Parag hits out at commentators for off-field antics: Talk only about cricketRiyan Parag has urged commentators to stop personal criticism of IPL players, saying athletes are human too, while also praising 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's maturity after his stunning 93.

Bangladesh teammates troll Rizwan with Bollywood jibesBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Mohammad Rizwan and Litton Das traded sharp words on Day 4 of the Sylhet Test, which Pakistan lost by 73 runs. Bangladesh's stump-mic Bollywood jibes went viral on social media.

Another Banglawash: Pakistan sink to new low despite Rizwan fight in Sylhet TestBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Bangladesh outclassed Pakistan by 78 runs in Sylhet on Wednesday to complete a 2-0 Test sweep. It is the second successive 0-2 defeat for Pakistan against Bangladesh as they slipped to No. 8 in the World Test Championship cycle.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reveals meaning behind 'A' celebration that's gone viralVaibhav Sooryavanshi revealed the secret behind his new 'A' celebration which he brought out after his half-century against the Lucknow Super Giants in Jaipur.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
